reviews.review_msg: Recommended
Deniz is so good and he is willing to provide additional office hour to help students.

Deniz is a great professor that is super knowledgeable. His assignments are pretty tough, so make sure to ask questions during office hours. His midterm was pretty easy and VERY similar to his sample exam. However, the content after the midterm got much more difficult and the final was pretty brutal. Didn't study the textbook at all, only lectures.

Deniz was a great professor and his exams were pretty easy. Just do his practice exams and you will find most of the content on the exam on there. Pretty knowledgeable guy with a finance background in New York.

Very nice guy and receptive to feedback - can tell he genuinely cares about students' learning. 

Class is out of 110%, the extra 10% is participation 

Not the best teacher, goes off on tangents relating to theory that are not directly testable, and doesn't make that clear. 

Class is hard - not his fault, just difficult content.
Pay attention in class, study the homework and class examples and you will do fine. It's worth participating in class as you get extra marks.

He was personable and receptive to feedback, but his teaching lacked effectiveness. Lectures veered off on non‑testable theory, skipped core concepts, and frequently backtracked, muddling the message. Despite studying the text and problem sets, exam content exceeded the syllabus, and grades for assignments 2 and 3 remain unreleased.

it was his first semester teaching so there were a few kinks but was but he was committed to students learning and incorporating our feedback, the midterm was difficult but similar in comparison to other finance classes study the assignments/excel and you'll be fine, overall khalil was very caring and personable

His lectures are quite boring. He's a little unorganized. His midterm was a bit difficult, requiring a thorough understanding of the materials. Additionally, he tends to include Excel questions in both the midterm and assignments.
Jacky is very knowledgeable and responsible. Investment game project was very cool. Willing to accommodate my request for extra office hours. Definitely one of the better lectures.

The man cannot teach if his life depended on it. He rambles on incessantly about things that are irrelevant, which means that even the (painful) 4 hours of class are never enough for him to finish explaining a single chapter. He had a bunch of errors in the exam which made the questions not make sense and then just apologized when it was all over

He was a nice guy, but he couldn't explain things clearly. However, the overall course was easy as long as you have done the assignments and studied the lecture slides (the exams would be very similar with the exercises and slides). Also, Jacky would bring some finance professionals to share their success and experiences.
Great Prof, very responsible and with strong investment knowledge. Enjoyed the guest speakers a lot. Assignments are a bit too many.
This is not a Finance class, he focus on key words and his experience. He makes lots of mistake during lecture and never clarify his mistake.
He is nice, but he just can't explain things clearly. Some people said his course was easy. I had a difficult time in understanding his lecture slides and his assignments.

You will need to put in effort for this course. He may seem strict at first, but once you get to know him he is very helpful. The final/midterm used questions from tutorials so go to them, and the final was easier than the in-class midterm.
nice and friendly. final exam was easier than the midterm. This is true, use tutorial questions for study.

Good prof. I like how he explained complicated formulas and some stats concepts. The marking was fair. Take home exam is long but we had enough time. The other exams are based on the tutorial questions, so get a copy of the answers.
He is always focusing on his stocks and phd rather than this course.
Worst instructor I've ever met at SFU!!! He is not responsible at all to his students.
This prof does not give out answer keys to his assignments. making those who didn't do too well on the take home exam or assignment hard to pull up their marks later in the course.

Frederick is the greatest professor I met at SFU. He is sooooooooo nice and patient, and he explains the concept clearly. If you ask him questions by email, he responds quickly and clearly. For the online course, we only get weekly quizzes, assignments and one report. Also, he gives fair marks. I highly recommend you take his course!

Despite the course being quite technical, Yuriy made this class very interesting! As long as you pay attention in class and ensure you understand the homework questions, you will be fine. His exams are pretty difficult, but nothing crazy if you put the work in.

I took BUS 315 with yuriy and he was a great prof. His class was engaging and his explanations were clear. His final exam was straightforward, and he went over similar questions in class. If Yuriy ever teaches a 400 level finance course, I will take it with him. Highly recommend

Hes a smart guy for sure, abundance of knowledge inside of his brain. However, he has an unorthadox way of teaching. His notes only have questions and doesnt have the solved answer. He tends to use a lot of real life examples but wont allow for an extensive formula sheet during exams. He picks a couple to have on there. Okay but not the best prof.
He is nice, approachable and handsome :) Explain concepts very clearly. However, the tests are a bit heavy and long, especially the final.

I took his Bus315 prior to taking my CFA level 1. There was a lot of correspondence between what we learned in class and what was on the actual CFA exam. Never had problems getting help from him as he was always willing to help after class or during office hours. Final was challenging. Since I was preparing for both exams well I got A.
No Comments
goat prof just go to class answer a few questions and the rest is so easy. incredible professor. just have to go to lectures.
She is okay

If you're looking to damage your GPA, take his class. Majority failed his mid-term! Had to re-weight the final to adjust for it! Old confusing teacher! You will learn zero about capital markets & be baffled with far too in depth stats!

Got to be the most confusing professor ever. Notes, assignments, and exams are not connected. Exam questions are a surprise every time, and is super confusing when lecturing. Average for midterm was 47%

Grauer will emphasize the material is hard but will not put any effort in simplifying the material. He does NOT provide any calculation examples even though 80%-90% of homework, midterm, exam are calculation based. The way he will teach you finance is by reading off the solution to homework questions and read off formula from notes.

Lectures and homework have almost nothing to do with what's on the final exam. There's no way to study for this course, as no practice final was given. Grauer is a nice guy but he isn't a good prof, he thinks every student can understand the concepts and be able to use them to solve unfamiliar questions, which is not fair.

Although I respect Rob's rich personality. His lectures get Boring every now and then. The textbook is necessary but not the best option. Look for other sources. Study every week and do all of the problems and you'll succeed.
His exams were hard and tricky and you had to put more efforts if you would like to get high marks.

EXTREMELY nice guy, but his lectures don't coincide with the textbook readings. Notations are different in lectures than in the textbook, and you have to do a lot of figuring out of assignments on your own. Midterm gets very tricky, and so does the final. Go to class, stay on top of the homework, and most importantly, try to UNDERSTAND the material

Lots of interesting stories. A good prof but tends to get off topic sometimes. Exams were not unfair but there will be one question on every exam that is difficult and forces you to think outside the box.
Easy credits if you've taken an actual stats course before.  Grauer has lots of interesting stories and anecdotes to talk about, as he was there when everything happened.

Rob is one of the better professors in the business faculty, he kept me interested throughout the course despite some dull material. The exams seemed to many to be unfair but I did not think either was particularly outside the norm. To do well you must apply yourself to the material throughout the course. I would gladly take another class with him.

Rob is a really genuinely nice man, but can be very confusing and has a strong tendency to go off track. Proving the formulas in class ends up wasting a lot of time, and more examples would have been useful. The textbook is great, and really needed as the power point notes miss a lot as 315 covers so much!
final is really brutal...materials that are not covered in the class was also tested and it was not from the text either...

HORRIBLE! his lectures have very little to do with material. mostly on his own research which is irrelevent.refuses to do problems in class & will get it wrong if he does do it. Tests = extremely hard& come from nowhere. Passing mark for midterm was 39% & he still wouldnt go over the test w/ the class!

He knows very well about FIN stuff. MT was actually not hard but final WAS hard. No formula sheet for exam but I find tats a good thing coz this forces u to try to understand what u r learning. Do the hwk MANY times & u'll be fine. Take his class coz some other profs are really xxxx, he is better...
Clearly a smart guy. Focusing alot on math proof's of finance theories. Doesn't use formula sheets on exam so expect a long exam, not to indepth, and lower averages.
No Comments
No Comments

Text book will be your bestfriend, dont even bother attending lectures he makes so many mistakes he'll end up confusing you more than he can teach you. Go to TA for help instead, he's useless. Exams are mostly fair, ~ 2/6 questions are tricky cos there's always keeners in the class
nice prof and help me take useful notes. I just take notes and understand what he talks, then receive a high mark. The questions in exam are mostly what he had talked.
Good Prof easy A+

Terrible professor. He was disorganized and all over the place. Learned everything from the text book. He makes endless mistakes during lecture and his notes are extremely repetitive. Do not take this course with this professor if you cant learn on your own.

He has got to be the worst professor at SFU. lecture notes are sloppy and all over the place, and he tends to just derive formulas all class. Assignments deviate from class notes and examples heavily, which makes reading through all the chapters in the text necessary. No practice questions that are similar to the midterm or final are given BAD PROF

Disorganized, messy writing, and just an overall bad teacher. Course outline doesn`t even have the required reading for the week. He wastes time deriving formulas and the assignments are so much harder than the examples in the notes and the text. If you can learn on your own than take this clown. I can say he`s one of the worst profs ever.

He will only trying to memorize his notes, and then write it down on the overhead, and he has problems with scrolling the overhead. HE ALWAYS makes errors in writing... and he always write the words together, which is hard to read his writing, even worse than 312 prof. always read the book yourself, otherwise you wont understand..

Not organized (does not know the university schedule), his notes don't coincide with assignments (therefore you are assigned material not yet covered), no schedule of required readings, frequent mistakes in overhead slides, no replies to emails.

Extremely disorganized. Ex. Had no idea there was a reading week break. Starts off a class with approx. 30min. recap of the previous lecture, which is not necessary. Assigns assignments for topics that have not yet been covered in class. Do yourself a favor and take this class with someone else.

WORST PROFF EVER! His is highly disorganized and keeps going off topic. He spends ridiculous amount of time on calculations and deriving formulas but is unable to explain the concepts. Make sure to read the textbook as his notes are hard to follow. During the lecture all you do is copy the notes that he writes on the overhead. AVOID AT ALL COSTS!!!

Unclear, disorganized notes. Basically, his job is to copy stuff from the textbook. It is extremely hard to read his notes. His manner is not good during his office hour. However, I somehow find this course easy... just an average prof.

I was irritated by how disrespectful I felt when I went to his office hour and asked questions. He had no patience to help me understand where I got wrong, but just kept telling me this was how it was. Super unorganized notes. I learned everything by reading the textbook.

Wikipedia is more useful than him. His lectures and notes are very disorganized and incomprehensible, and he makes bad jokes all through his lectures. For many occasions I have to rely on Wiki to understand the material. Nice guy by appearance, but not useful.
always attend his lecture or else you are left behind. A lot of notes at least 4 pages per class. His notes sometimes repetitive, textbook is really helpful. Exams are fair.

He is extremely unhelpful, unorganized and rude to students. He thinks that he knows everything and doesn't understand why people ask him stupid questions. The WORST prof ever! Avoid him at all costs.

he's a nice guy and has fair exams. He may be a bit unorganized in class but by the end of the day if u pay attention and study his notes, u'll do just fine. I hate those who don't respect the prof by leaving before he finishes talking. I can tell he's not happy with those ****s but never said anything about it. Poor guy :(
great professor! very helpfull and knowledgable! exams are easy if you understand his notes! looks nice.

This prof is pretty nice but he is super unorganized. He doesn't post slides and makes his students to take notes by hand! The course material isn't too difficult but I thought the prof was pretty bad.
He's very passionate about Finance. Exams are fair, notes are clear and more importantly it's understandable. Pretty much a great prof except he replies to e-mail only sometimes if it's important.

I did very well on the midterm, so I'm not someone who is complaining because they did poorly, but he has to be one of the worst teacher's I ever had at SFU.  The guy literally just copies his notes out of his book, and you just sit their copying his without learning.  A perfect example of why this school is a waste of money. Avoid him at all costs

His notes are easier to understand than the textbook, don't need the text except for hw. Exams fair, based on the hw. Sometimes unknowingly repeats last class notes verbatim so pay attention to save yourself some effort. At the end of the day he is a good guy and passionate about his field. You'll do fine if you come to class and copy his notes.

EASY as hell if you can do basic math. No stupid math proofs. Just easy calculation. No need to read book. I got A+. Just get answer key to book and do all questions. Took me 2 days to study for each exam while not attending class

Did pretty good in his course. But he wastes your time a lot. He keeps repeating himself over and over again, but you have to go to his lectures, since all his questions on midterms and final are the examples he showed in class. Do his practice exams and understand the concepts on those questions, then you'll do fine.

Very unhelpful. Teaching is not his priority (though he knows what he's doing) and it shows. If you understand the concepts well, u'll do average. Its a hard course and the prof isn't a good one either.
Notes are very important.
Not very clear on lecture and always give wrong calculation, but the mid-term is ok. The assignments are pretty hard but my T.A (Jacky Lui) is very helpful.
no preparation for any lectures at all. no answer keys given to practice questions. confusing grading system..need to say more?
DO NOT TAKE, doesn't really teach you anything, have to learn everything on your own. No practice during the class, if it delays your graduation let it, not worth taking the course with her.

DO NOT TAKE DO NOT TAKE DO NOT TAKE. I have never written a review or even look at this site but I have to for this. She is by FAR the worst prof in existence. Ive never been so worried about failing a class. These aren't hard concepts she it just brutal at teaching and testing. She makes a mid difficult class a TRAMA-INDUCED NIGHTMARE. NO!
Slides are awful, unclear what is being taught. RUN RUN RUN

Her teaching skills are completely inadequate. Laying the PPT out on the table and just reading it without any interaction with the students. If you unfortunately take her course, you can only pray for the best.
She made the already difficult course even worse. Don't choose her section at all.

Among all the courses in Finance, this one is the most difficult. She simply had no idea what she was doing in class. The students kept reading the PPT and didn't respond. So I decided to skip a chapter and keep adding more content, not following the prescribed chapters as stated in the syllabus. Terrible!
She doesn't know how to teach, 1 session have 3-4 chapters, good luck
Don't take her course, run!!!!!
She is so terrible, I never thought a course have that much chapters to learn. Avoid

Just did her exam. Guy sitting behind me lost 2 years of his life. The TA Terrell didn't help anyone when they had a question except on girl. What's the point of him even being there if he can't clarify questions.
Just finished her final. I think I lost around 2 years of my life expectancy.

By FAR the worst professor I've ever had. Mumbles in a small class of 40 students, and when you ask her to speak up she doesn't. Doesn't teach anything, puts up slides (which make 0 sense) and reads it WORD FOR WORD. Quizzes are 30 questions in 30 mins. Actually so mind blown by HOW BADDDDD SHE IS I AM IN SHOCK.

Please do yourself a favour by not taking her class. As excited as I was when I took the class, I've lost the excitement, motivation, and even the courage to take her exam. I'm thinking about switching concentrations, my mind can't think straight about finance, the same concentration I was looking forward to studying for years. HORRIBLE!
IDK is this course hard, or you make it that hard. Greta, please bless us and retire. No offense

She does not care about her students, she does the bare minimum and if anyone doesn't understand a concept she blames it on them. It is nearly impossible to follow what she is saying during lectures as she speaks extremely quickly and quietly. At times it almost seems like she doesn't even know what she is saying. Stay away.

For a professor to be teaching a course for this many years with no improvement in course delivery, SFU needs to fire her. It is unfair that students continue to suffer from her lectures consisting of reading slides and whispering, along with formulas with no explanation in application. Avoid her at all costs.
Greta if your reading this. Please bless the students and retire.

The first 1-star review was on August 18, 2010. You would think that a professor would make adjustments. 15 years later, students are still in the same situation... SFU, please sit in on one of her lectures, and I guarantee that you will leave a bad review yourself.

I really wish Sfu reads these comments. Greta doesn't teach, she reads off the slides. If a student asks her a question, she doesn't answer the question but goes off topic with another answer. Even if she is your only option, DO NOT TAKE HER!. Even if it will delay your graduation, DO NOT TAKE HER!
I can't describe the number of times I wished I had a different professor for this class. The ratings speak for themself. 30 1/5 ratings. That 5/5 rating is not true, don't listen to it.

Worst teacher ive ever had at SFU. Whispers in class, and when someone called her out she told them to sit closer. Slides are horrible, as formulas are just given with no legend explaining what variables mean, or even what the formula is used for. SFU needs to re-evaluate her as a teacher here.
Currently studying for her final right now, and it made me want to write another review. DON'T TAKE HER. I wish I had taken this class in another semester.

Completely self-learning class. She's basically whispering during her lecture which makes it near impossible to understand what she's saying. The classroom is basically a tutorial sized classroom. And she thought it was a good idea to have an assignment due on the 9th when people have finals to study for.
terrible. straight up terrible. might have to switch concentrations cause of her.
Is unable to teach the concepts clearly. Self learning class.
Worst Lecturer I've ever had. Avoid at all cost.
This class single handedly destroyed my will to live
Would NOT recommend.

Hardly complain about profs, but Greta is brutal. Very difficult to understand, not because of an accent but a horrendous ability to explain concepts. Spends years on pointless terms then whips through the mathematical portions. It's a self-learning class. Her TA Terrell is an inaccurate marker.  Pros- Straightforward exams, tries(ish) to help.
Greta is a nice person, but her lectures are sometimes hard to understand because she speaks fast. However, the averages are low so it is not hard to beat them.
Very boring and unnecessary lectures. Does provide past tests so easy to teach yourself the material. You wont need the textbook. There are probably better options.

She is really nice and gentle! Although her ppt looks like a bit colorful, but once you familiar with this format you will find those colors actually help you understand. Also, she will provide enough practice and sample before test, which are quite helpful. She is also willing to answer questions and explained very clear.

She is nice and willing to help students but she puts way too much info on each slide. It is a four hour lecture and theres way too much info to absorb. And its really confusing, you have to spend time organizing it
No Comments
His accent is overpowering.  Writes in another language, other than numbers.  Ends most sentences with  right right .  FINAL is 10x harder than MT.  NOT a good prof at all.
his TA did a better job...
overhead is too small...
No Comments
Must read ahead to do well in this course with him teaching.
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments

Ying is one of the best professors at sfu. Her homework is challenging, but her grading policy is lenient. She is patient to students, and her teaching is very clear and easy to follow. All details are clearly presented on the slides. Most of the bad ratings are due to the tough assignments, but you can learn a lot from these assignments.
great prof, take this class with him if you were good at bus315&316
If you really want to learn something meat and real finance stuff, take with Prof. Yee.  Knowledgeable. Accessible. Encouraging. Patient. Very nice professor.

He was good at explaining concepts, but at times his notes are way too colorful. Biggest problem I had was that the questions he would assign and give answers to were very easy, in other words, it was hard to prepare for tests that were actually difficult, such as his mid-term and final. Tough to do well b/c of lack of ability to prepare for exams

I took BUS315 with Professor Yee. He is always there for students who have questions. I asked so many questions through the whole semester and many of them are repeated until I understand. Professor Yee was always patient and asked my questions again and again. His lecture notes are super clear. I am so glad to attend lectures with him.
